node_modules.explanation: Files downloaded automatically for a coding project. Nothing important is stored here and they can always be recreated.

DerivedData.explanation: Temporary files created by Xcode while building apps. Safe to delete, your next build will just take a little longer.

iOS DeviceSupport.explanation: Files downloaded to support older iPhones connected to your Mac. Safe to remove if you no longer use those devices.

spotify.explanation: Spotify stores music data here to load songs faster. Deleting it just means Spotify will reload things a bit slower for a while.

chrome.explanation: Chrome saves website data here to load pages faster. Safe to delete and will rebuild automatically as you browse.

safari.explanation: Safari stores temporary website files here. Safe to clear. Pages may take a moment longer to load after.

firefox.explanation: Firefox saves website data to load pages faster. Safe to delete and will rebuild automatically as you browse.

slack.explanation: Slack stores messages and images locally to load faster. Your actual messages are safe in the cloud.

figma.explanation: Figma stores project previews and assets here. Safe to delete. Figma will reload your files from the cloud next time.

zoom.explanation: Temporary files created by Zoom for meetings. Safe to delete, nothing important is stored here.

notion.explanation: Notion stores pages and content locally to load faster. Your actual data is safely stored in the cloud.

discord.explanation: Discord saves images and data locally to load faster. Safe to delete, everything will reload from Discord's servers.

whatsapp.explanation: WhatsApp stores temporary data to load chats faster. Your messages are stored on your phone and in the app, not here.

telegram.explanation: Telegram stores media locally to load faster. Your actual messages are safe in the cloud.

dropbox.explanation: Temporary files Dropbox uses to sync your files. Deleting may cause Dropbox to re-sync some files but your actual files in the cloud are not affected.

googledrive.explanation: Temporary files Google Drive uses to keep your files synced. Your files in the cloud are safe but Drive may need to re-sync after.

onedrive.explanation: Temporary files used by Microsoft OneDrive to sync your documents. Your cloud files are safe but OneDrive will re-sync after.

icloud.explanation: Temporary files used by iCloud to sync your files and photos. Your data in iCloud is safe but syncing may take a moment to restart.

vscode.explanation: Temporary files created by Visual Studio Code. Safe to delete and VS Code will recreate them next time it opens.

cursor.explanation: Temporary files created by the Cursor code editor. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you open Cursor again.

xcode.explanation: Cache files used by Xcode. Deleting may slow down your first build after but nothing will be permanently lost.

ollama.explanation: Temporary cache files used by Ollama. Your actual AI models are stored separately and are not affected. Safe to delete.

docker.explanation: Temporary cache files used by Docker. Your actual Docker images and containers are stored separately and are not affected.

pnpm.explanation: A cache of downloaded coding packages. Safe to delete and will be rebuilt automatically next time a project needs them.

npm.explanation: A cache of downloaded coding packages. Safe to delete. Packages will just be re-downloaded next time they are needed.

yarn.explanation: A cache of downloaded coding packages managed by Yarn. Safe to delete and will rebuild automatically.

homebrew.explanation: Leftover installation files from Homebrew, a tool developers use to install software. Safe to delete. Your installed apps will still work fine.

gradle.explanation: Temporary files used when building Android or Java projects. Safe to remove and will be re-downloaded when next needed.

cocoapods.explanation: Cached files for a tool used in iOS app development. Safe to delete and will be recreated when needed.

rust.explanation: Build output from Rust coding projects. Auto-generated and can be recreated by building the project again.

target.explanation: Build output from Rust coding projects. Auto-generated and can be recreated by building the project again.

python.explanation: Temporary files created by Python to run code faster. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

pip.explanation: Cached Python packages. Safe to delete and packages will just be re-downloaded next time they are needed.

venv.explanation: A local setup for a Python project. Safe to delete if the project is not active. Running the project setup again will recreate it.

Pods.explanation: Downloaded dependency code for Apple platform projects using CocoaPods. You can recreate it later by installing dependencies again.

flutter.explanation: Generated files Flutter uses while building an app. They can grow large and usually come back automatically on your next build.

photos.explanation: Thumbnail and preview data for your photo library. Safe to delete but the Photos app will need time to rebuild previews, which can take a while for large libraries.

mail.explanation: Locally cached email data. Deleting means Mail will need to re-download your emails from the server, which may take some time.

music.explanation: Temporary files stored by Apple Music to load songs faster. Safe to delete. Your library and downloads are stored separately.

maps.explanation: Map tile data stored locally to load maps faster. Safe to delete. Maps will just re-download tiles as you use it.

geoservices.explanation: Temporary location data stored by macOS to load maps and location features faster. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

microsoft.explanation: Temporary files created by Microsoft apps like Word, Excel, Outlook, or Edge. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you open the apps.

teams.explanation: Temporary files stored by Microsoft Teams. Safe to delete. Your chats and files are stored in the cloud.

linear.explanation: Temporary files stored by Linear, a project management app. Your actual data is safely stored in the cloud.

arc.explanation: Arc browser saves website data here to load pages faster. Safe to delete and will rebuild as you browse.

brave.explanation: Brave browser saves website data here to load pages faster. Safe to delete and will rebuild automatically.

opera.explanation: Opera saves website data locally to load pages faster. Safe to delete and will rebuild as you browse.

1password.explanation: Temporary data stored by 1Password. Your actual passwords are safely encrypted and stored separately. Deleting may require you to sign in again.

alfred.explanation: Temporary files stored by Alfred, a Mac productivity tool. Safe to delete and Alfred will rebuild its cache automatically.

raycast.explanation: Temporary files stored by Raycast, a Mac productivity tool. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

iterm.explanation: Temporary files stored by iTerm, a terminal app for Mac. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you open iTerm.

sketch.explanation: Temporary files stored by Sketch, a design app. Safe to delete. Your actual design files are not stored here.

loom.explanation: Temporary files stored by Loom for video recording and playback. Safe to delete. Your recorded videos are stored in the cloud.

grammarly.explanation: Temporary files used by Grammarly. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically when you use Grammarly.

lungo.explanation: Temporary files stored by Lungo, a small app that keeps your Mac awake. Safe to delete.

cleanmymac.explanation: Temporary files stored by CleanMyMac itself. Safe to delete.

comet.explanation: Temporary files stored by Comet. Safe to delete and will be recreated when the app is used again.

dia.explanation: Temporary files stored by Dia. Safe to delete and will be recreated when the app is used again.

granola.explanation: Temporary files stored by Granola, an AI meeting notes app. Your actual notes are stored in the cloud.

claude.explanation: Temporary files stored by the Claude desktop app. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you open Claude again.

perplexity.explanation: Temporary files stored by Perplexity AI. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you use the app again.

openai.explanation: Temporary files stored by OpenAI tools. Safe to delete and will be recreated when needed.

blender.explanation: Temporary files created by Blender, a 3D design tool. Safe to delete. Your actual Blender project files are not stored here.

sentry.explanation: Temporary files used by Sentry, an error tracking tool used by developers. Safe to delete.

datadog.explanation: Temporary files used by Datadog, a monitoring tool used by developers. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

segment.explanation: Temporary files used by Segment, an analytics tool used by apps in the background. Safe to delete.

revenuecat.explanation: Temporary files used by RevenueCat, a payment and subscription tool used by apps. Safe to delete.

spokenly.explanation: Temporary files stored by Spokenly. Safe to delete and will be recreated when the app is used again.

swiftpm.explanation: Cached Swift packages downloaded for coding projects. Safe to delete and will be re-downloaded when needed.

gamekit.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple Game Center. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically when you use Game Center.

cloudkit.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple CloudKit to sync app data. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically when apps sync.

imagecache.explanation: Cached image data stored by apps to load images faster. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

passkit.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple Wallet. Safe to delete. Your actual passes are stored in iCloud and will reload automatically.

homekit.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple Home for smart home devices. Safe to delete but your Home app may take a moment to reconnect to devices.

spotlight.explanation: Temporary files used by Spotlight search. Safe to delete and Spotlight will rebuild its index automatically.

appstore.explanation: Temporary files used by the Mac App Store. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

appleaccount.explanation: Temporary files used by your Apple account to stay signed in. Safe to delete but you may need to sign in to some Apple services again.

siri.explanation: Temporary files used by Siri to process requests faster. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

findmy.explanation: Temporary files used by Find My. Safe to delete. Your device locations are tracked by Apple and will reload automatically.

google.explanation: Temporary files used by Google tools and updaters. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

jetbrains.explanation: Temporary files used by JetBrains IDEs like IntelliJ or WebStorm. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you open the IDE.

playwright.explanation: Cached browser files used by Playwright, a testing tool for developers. Safe to delete and will be re-downloaded when needed.

nsurlsession.explanation: Temporary network data cached by macOS to speed up connections. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

reminders.explanation: Temporary files used by the Reminders app. Safe to delete. Your actual reminders are stored in iCloud and will sync back.

tips.explanation: Temporary files used by the macOS Tips app. Safe to delete.

translation.explanation: Temporary files used by the macOS Translation feature to speed up translations. Safe to delete and will be recreated when you use translation again.

shazam.explanation: Temporary files used by Shazam music recognition. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

videoconference.explanation: Temporary files used by FaceTime and video calling on your Mac. Safe to delete.

purge.explanation: Temporary files created by Purge itself. Safe to delete.

ntware.explanation: Temporary files stored by NT-ware SmartClient, a print management tool. Safe to delete.

archiveutility.explanation: Temporary files created when opening or creating zip files and archives. Safe to delete.

familycircle.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple Family Sharing. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

zen.explanation: Temporary files stored by Zen Browser. Safe to delete and will rebuild as you browse.

helpd.explanation: Temporary files used by the macOS Help system. Safe to delete.

ampagent.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple media services for Music, TV, and Podcasts. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

containermanager.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to manage app sandboxing. Safe to delete and will be recreated automatically.

duetexpert.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to learn your usage patterns and improve suggestions. Safe to delete.

featureaccess.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to manage which features your apps can access. Safe to delete.

cachedelete.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to manage and clean up system caches. Safe to delete.

dataaccess.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to sync contacts, calendars, and other data. Safe to delete.

parsecd.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS to power Siri suggestions and proactive features. Safe to delete.

chrono.explanation: Temporary files used by Screen Time on your Mac. Safe to delete.

feedbacklogger.explanation: Temporary files used by Apple Feedback Assistant for submitting bug reports. Safe to delete.

betaenrollment.explanation: Temporary files used to manage macOS beta program enrollment. Safe to delete.

donotdisturb.explanation: Temporary files used by macOS Focus and Do Not Disturb modes. Safe to delete.

Library/Preferences.explanation: These files store your personal settings for apps. Deleting them will reset apps back to their defaults and you may lose your configurations.

Library/Keychains.explanation: This folder contains your saved passwords and login credentials. Do not delete this as it cannot be recovered.

Library/Application Support.explanation: Important data that apps need to function, including saved progress and local databases. Deleting the wrong files here can break apps.
